pages.raw_text:
The Press today gives the women
of this city and community the first
opportunity they have had to vote
on the great question: DO you
want to vote at all elections on an
equality with mem"


Presented herewith is ballot
which our woman readers may fill
out, clip and return to the Suffrage
Editor, to be counted on election
day.


For the first time in 25 years the
question of amending the constitu-
ton to enable women to vote Is up
to congress.


The National American Woman
Suffrage association presented to
congress April 10 petition of
nearly a half million names which
demands that the national law max
ing body submit to the legislatures
of the several states an amendment
to the national constitution which
shall enable women to vote.


Do the women of Spokane want
congress to pass such a resolution?


Those who do will put cross in
the space marked YES on the &C.
companying ballot; those who don't
will put cross in the space labeled
NO.


For years we've discussed the
question. Now let's find out if the
women WANT to vote


We introduce Mrs. Oliver Fl. p.
Belmont of New York, who at the
editors request has consented to
open the campaign from The Press
platform. See page Z
